project.name: finapp
project.description: Open-source personal finance app — offline-first PWA with real-time sync. Track expenses, income, transfers, and wallets across multiple currencies.

nodes.name: server.js
nodes.summary: Auto-generated Convex server utilities that re-export typed wrappers (query, mutation, action and their internal variants) from the convex/server package for use across all backend function files.
nodes.languageNotes: Generated by `npx convex dev`; do not edit manually. Provides the typed builder functions all other Convex files depend on.

nodes.name: categories.ts
nodes.summary: Convex backend module implementing full CRUD and hierarchical management for user categories, including multi-page transaction removal and recursive child updates with tombstone tracking.

nodes.name: shared.ts
nodes.summary: Shared Convex backend utilities providing validation helpers (string length, number range), domain constants (date and amount bounds), auth accessors, and a generic ownership-checked entity loader.

nodes.name: tombstones.ts
nodes.summary: Convex backend module managing soft-delete tombstone records for offline sync, providing helpers to emit single or bulk tombstones and an internal scheduled action to purge entries older than 30 days.

nodes.name: trns.ts
nodes.summary: Convex backend module implementing full CRUD, real-time delta sync, hash-based integrity checks, and sync metadata management for financial transactions (trns), supporting regular, transfer, and adjustment transaction types.

nodes.name: user.ts
nodes.summary: Convex backend module managing user settings (get/upsert) and providing an action to remove all user data by delegating to the migrate module.

nodes.name: updateCategoryCore
nodes.summary: Core category update helper that validates and patches category fields (name, icon, color, parentId) while enforcing ownership, preventing circular parent references, and checking name uniqueness per parent.

nodes.name: validateStringLength
nodes.summary: Throws an error if a string value exceeds the specified maximum character length; silently passes for undefined values.

nodes.name: validateNumberRange
nodes.summary: Throws an error if a numeric value falls outside the inclusive [min, max] range; silently passes for undefined values.

nodes.name: getAuthUser
nodes.summary: Returns the authenticated user from the Better Auth component or null if unauthenticated; safe for use in read-only queries.

nodes.name: requireAuthUser
nodes.summary: Returns the authenticated user or throws an Unauthorized error; intended for mutations and actions that require authentication.

nodes.name: getOwnEntity
nodes.summary: Generic ownership-checked entity loader that fetches any user-owned table row by ID and throws if the record is missing or belongs to a different user.

nodes.name: emitTombstone
nodes.summary: Inserts a single tombstone record marking a deleted entity for offline sync, recording the entity type, ID, userId, and deletion timestamp.

nodes.name: emitTombstones
nodes.summary: Bulk-inserts tombstone records for a list of deleted entity IDs, sharing a single timestamp across all entries for consistency.

nodes.name: validateTrnFields
nodes.summary: Validates all fields of a transaction before create/update operations, checking string lengths, numeric ranges, ownership of wallets and categories, and enforces transfer-specific constraints for transfer-to wallet and amount.

nodes.name: api.js
nodes.summary: Auto-generated Convex API manifest exporting the typed `api`, `internal`, and `components` references used throughout the backend to call queries, mutations, and actions by reference.

nodes.name: crons.ts
nodes.summary: Registers Convex cron jobs: a daily exchange-rate fetch at 06:00 UTC and a daily tombstone purge at 04:00 UTC.

nodes.name: migrate.ts
nodes.summary: Provides internal Convex mutations and actions for bulk data migration: paginated deletion of user records across all app and auth tables, and batch insertion of wallets, categories, transactions, and user settings.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Convex `internalMutation`/`internalAction` to keep these operations private; deletion loops page through 500-record batches until the table is clear.

nodes.name: rates.ts
nodes.summary: Fetches and persists currency exchange rates from Coinbase (primary, ~960 currencies including crypto) and OpenExchangeRates (secondary, ~173 fiat currencies), exposing a public query to retrieve the latest merged rates.
nodes.languageNotes: Rates from both sources are stored separately by `source` key and merged at read time so the freshest entry for each currency wins.

nodes.name: trnsHash.ts
nodes.summary: Maintains a rolling FNV-1a XOR hash of all transaction IDs per user in `syncMeta`, enabling lightweight client-server sync verification without transferring the full transaction list.
nodes.languageNotes: FNV-1a is used with XOR so that adding and removing the same ID is symmetric - duplicated from app-side utils/fnv1a.ts because the Convex bundler cannot share modules with the frontend.

nodes.name: fetchFromCoinbase
nodes.summary: Fetches USD-denominated exchange rates from the Coinbase API and returns a parsed numeric rate map, or null on failure.

nodes.name: fetchFromOER
nodes.summary: Fetches fiat exchange rates from OpenExchangeRates using the OPEN_EXCHANGE_RATES_KEY env var, returning the rates map or null on failure.

nodes.name: fnv1aNum
nodes.summary: Computes a 32-bit unsigned FNV-1a hash of a string; exported so callers can independently compute the same hash value for a transaction ID.

nodes.name: getOrCreateSyncMeta
nodes.summary: Looks up or lazily creates the `syncMeta` record for a given user, ensuring the document exists before hash operations.

nodes.name: toggleTrnHash
nodes.summary: XOR-toggles a single transaction ID's FNV-1a hash into or out of the user's running `trnsIdsHash` in `syncMeta`.

nodes.name: toggleTrnsHash
nodes.summary: XOR-toggles a batch of transaction IDs into the user's running `trnsIdsHash` in a single `syncMeta` patch.

nodes.name: auth.config.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the Convex auth configuration by registering the Better Auth config provider as the sole authentication provider.

nodes.name: auth.ts
nodes.summary: Sets up the Better Auth instance for the Convex backend, configuring Google OAuth, cross-domain sessions, encrypted tokens, and a 30-day session lifetime.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the @convex-dev/better-auth adapter pattern: authComponent holds the Convex-side client, while createAuth is called per-request to produce a fully configured betterAuth instance with context-bound database adapter.

nodes.name: createAuth
nodes.summary: Factory that constructs a configured betterAuth instance bound to a Convex request context, wiring in Google OAuth, cross-domain CORS, JWKS rotation, and session policy.

nodes.name: http.ts
nodes.summary: Defines and exports the Convex HTTP router, registering Better Auth routes with CORS enabled so the SPA frontend can reach auth endpoints across origins.
nodes.languageNotes: Convex requires a default export of an httpRouter from http.ts; this file serves as the sole HTTP entry point for the backend.

nodes.name: schema.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the full Convex database schema for finapp, declaring all tables (categories, wallets, trns, userSettings, rates, syncMeta, tombstones) with their field validators and indexes.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Convex's defineSchema/defineTable DSL with v.* validators and chained .index() calls for efficient indexed queries.

nodes.name: validators.ts
nodes.summary: Exports shared Convex validators and domain enums - the WalletType union validator and the TrnType enum (Expense=0, Income=1, Transfer=2) - used across schema and mutation handlers.
nodes.languageNotes: Builds a v.union() validator dynamically from the walletTypes const array, ensuring the validator and the TypeScript type stay in sync.

nodes.name: wallets.ts
nodes.summary: Convex backend module providing all CRUD operations for wallets, including real-time delta sync, paginated transaction cleanup on delete, and an action-driven cascading removal flow.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Convex's action + internalMutation pattern for paginated cascading deletes, looping over three wallet-related transaction indexes to safely remove all linked trns before deleting the wallet.

nodes.name: list
nodes.summary: Convex query that returns all wallets belonging to the authenticated user, or null if unauthenticated.

nodes.name: deltaRealtime
nodes.summary: Convex query returning incremental wallet changes and tombstones since a given timestamp, capped at 500 items, to support real-time offline-first sync.

nodes.name: create
nodes.summary: Convex mutation that validates and inserts a new wallet record for the authenticated user, enforcing required fields and max-length constraints.

nodes.name: update
nodes.summary: Convex mutation that patches an existing wallet's fields after validating ownership and input constraints, then bumps updatedAt.

nodes.name: updateOrder
nodes.summary: Convex mutation that bulk-updates the order field for multiple wallets, verifying ownership of each before patching.

nodes.name: removeTrnsByWalletPage
nodes.summary: Internal Convex mutation that paginates through transactions linked to a wallet via a specified index and deletes them in batches of 500, emitting tombstones and updating the trns hash.

nodes.name: removeWalletFinalize
nodes.summary: Internal Convex mutation that deletes the wallet document itself after all linked transactions have been removed, emitting a wallet tombstone.

nodes.name: remove
nodes.summary: Convex action orchestrating cascading wallet deletion: loops over all three transaction indexes calling removeTrnsByWalletPage until pagination is complete, then calls removeWalletFinalize.

nodes.name: .mcp.json
nodes.summary: MCP (Model Context Protocol) server configuration that registers available MCP servers for Claude Code integration in this project.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Project-specific instructions for Claude Code covering the tech stack (Nuxt 4, Vue 3, Convex, Better Auth), dev commands, code conventions, and Convex backend access patterns for the finapp monorepo.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: English project overview for Finapp covering features (finance tracking, analytics, offline sync), tech stack, monorepo structure, getting-started guide, and all available scripts across 21 sections.

nodes.name: README.ru.md
nodes.summary: Russian-language mirror of README.md providing the same project overview, features, tech stack, repository structure, and getting-started instructions for Russian-speaking contributors.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Root monorepo package.json that defines workspace-level scripts (dev, build, lint, test, docs) aggregating commands from the app and docs packages, engine requirements (Node >= 24), and shared devDependencies.
nodes.languageNotes: Private monorepo root package using pnpm workspaces; scripts delegate to package-specific commands via --filter or workspace protocol.

nodes.name: pnpm-workspace.yaml
nodes.summary: pnpm workspace configuration declaring the app/ and docs/ packages as workspace members and defining any dependency version overrides for the monorepo.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Environment variable template declaring the three required Convex-related variables: deployment identifier, WebSocket URL, and site URL.

nodes.name: .mcp.json
nodes.summary: MCP server configuration registering two development tools: the Nuxt UI remote HTTP server and a local Playwright browser automation server with a custom config path.
nodes.languageNotes: MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers declared here are used by AI coding assistants to inspect Nuxt UI docs and drive browser tests.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: English-language package readme for @finapp/app listing key development commands and deferring installation and setup details to the monorepo root README.

nodes.name: README.ru.md
nodes.summary: Russian-language mirror of the @finapp/app package readme, listing development commands and referencing the monorepo root README for full setup instructions.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest for @finapp/app defining version 7.12.5, all runtime dependencies (Nuxt 4, Vue 3, Convex, Pinia, ECharts, Better Auth), dev tooling, and scripts for development, build, testing, and E2E runs.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration that extends the Nuxt-generated tsconfig and enforces strict mode with lib-check skipping for faster CI.
nodes.languageNotes: Extending `.nuxt/tsconfig.json` means all Nuxt auto-import path aliases and generated types are inherited automatically.

nodes.name: vercel.json
nodes.summary: Vercel deployment configuration specifying pnpm install, the Nuxt SPA build command, the `.output/public` output directory, and a smart ignore command that skips rebuilds when only unrelated workspace files changed.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Root CSS entry point that imports Tailwind CSS, @nuxt/ui, theme, and utility stylesheets, defines custom dark-mode and hover variants, and applies base html/body styles including a z-index fix for Reka UI popper portals.

nodes.name: theme.css
nodes.summary: Defines the application color theme via CSS custom properties - chart palette, dark/light mode UI token overrides, static colors for expense/income visualization, custom breakpoints, and brand/primary font families.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Tailwind CSS v4 @theme and @theme static directives for token registration alongside classic CSS custom properties for runtime theme switching.

nodes.name: utility.css
nodes.summary: Declares reusable Tailwind CSS v4 @utility classes for interactive states, flex centering, page layout wrappers, bottom-sheet layout primitives, and a custom scrollbar style block.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Tailwind CSS v4 @utility directive to register named utility classes globally, covering bottom-sheet-specific layout patterns and a scrollerBlock webkit scrollbar customization.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Configuration file listing paths and patterns to exclude from the understand-anything analysis tool, similar to a .gitignore format.

nodes.name: app.config.ts
nodes.summary: Nuxt app configuration defining global UI component defaults and theme settings for @nuxt/ui v4, including calendar, modal, popover, toast, and color/radius theming.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Nuxt's defineAppConfig for type-safe runtime configuration accessible via useAppConfig().

nodes.name: app.vue
nodes.summary: Root Vue application component that sets up global head metadata, SEO meta, theme color, CSS variables for radius and primary color, toast config, and the auth guard.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses useHead and useSeoMeta from @unhead/vue via Nuxt auto-imports; injects dynamic CSS custom properties for theming.

nodes.name: emo.ts
nodes.summary: Provides emoji constants for success, error, and warning states plus a random emoji picker utility for use in toast notifications.

nodes.name: icons.js
nodes.summary: Static data file exporting a large list of icon names available for category and wallet icon selection throughout the app.

nodes.name: About.vue
nodes.summary: Simple about section component displaying app author and contributor credits using i18n translations.

nodes.name: Amount.vue
nodes.summary: Reusable monetary amount display component supporting multiple size variants, sign indicators, colorization by transaction type, and optional base-currency rate conversion row.

nodes.name: AmountItem.vue
nodes.summary: Presentational sub-component rendering a single formatted amount value with optional sign prefix and currency symbol, used internally by Amount.vue.

nodes.name: getTotal.ts
nodes.summary: Core financial calculation utilities: currency rate conversion, per-transaction-list income/expense/transfer totals, and single-pass O(N) wallet balance aggregation.
nodes.languageNotes: getWalletsTotals uses a single O(N) pass over transactions to compute per-wallet balances, avoiding repeated full scans.

nodes.name: getAmountInRate
nodes.summary: Converts a monetary amount from one currency to another using stored exchange rates; returns the original amount if rates or base currency are unavailable.

nodes.name: getTotal
nodes.summary: Iterates a list of transaction IDs and accumulates income, expense, transfer, and adjustment totals with optional wallet filtering and currency conversion.

nodes.name: getWalletsTotals
nodes.summary: Computes per-wallet balance totals from all transactions in a single O(N) pass, returning a Map of walletId to balance.

nodes.name: useAmount.ts
nodes.summary: Vue composable exposing base currency code, formatted base-rate conversion helper, and a computed transaction total calculator, bridging store data with amount utilities.

nodes.name: useAmount
nodes.summary: Returns reactive helpers for currency-aware amount formatting and total computation by connecting to currencies, wallets, and trns stores.

nodes.name: utils.ts
nodes.summary: Low-level amount formatting utilities: cached Intl.NumberFormat instances, significant-digit fallback formatting, currency symbol lookup, and thousand-separator formatting.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a module-level formatterCache Map to avoid recreating Intl.NumberFormat instances for the same precision.

nodes.name: formatAmount
nodes.summary: Formats a numeric amount with locale-aware thousand separators, applying significant-digit fallback when rounding would display zero for non-zero values.

nodes.name: Copyright.vue
nodes.summary: Footer attribution link to the author's website with an animated heartbeat icon, displayed via i18n strings.

nodes.name: Onboarding.vue
nodes.summary: Onboarding screen shown to new users, guiding them step-by-step to create their first wallet, category, or transaction with contextual call-to-action buttons.

nodes.name: useInitApp.ts
nodes.summary: Application initialization composable orchestrating data loading from localforage cache on startup, syncing from the Convex database after auth, replaying offline queues, and starting realtime subscriptions.
nodes.languageNotes: Manages the cache-first then DB-sync startup flow: loads all stores from localforage in parallel, then awaits Convex auth before fetching fresh data and replaying offline queue mutations.

nodes.name: useInitApp
nodes.summary: Initializes the app by loading cached data, setting up online/offline event listeners, loading fresh data from Convex, and starting realtime sync after authentication.

nodes.name: BottomSheet.vue
nodes.summary: Core bottom sheet component with drag-to-dismiss, body scroll lock, overlay opacity animation, and transition-aware class bindings powered by useBottomSheetDrag.

nodes.name: BottomSheetClose.vue
nodes.summary: Small close button rendered as an absolute-positioned circle in the top-right corner of a bottom sheet panel.

nodes.name: BottomSheetHandler.vue
nodes.summary: Minimal drag-handle indicator bar rendered at the top of a bottom sheet to signal that it is draggable.

nodes.name: BottomSheetModal.vue
nodes.summary: Convenience wrapper that teleports BottomSheet to the document body with standard handler and close button, exposing a default slot with a close callback.

nodes.name: BottomSheetOrDropdown.vue
nodes.summary: Adaptive container that renders a UPopover dropdown on laptop/mouse devices and a teleported BottomSheet on touch/mobile, with shared trigger and content slots.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses usePointer and useWindowSize to conditionally switch between Popover (desktop) and BottomSheet (mobile) layouts.

nodes.name: useBottomSheetDrag.ts
nodes.summary: Composable implementing touch and mouse drag mechanics for the bottom sheet: tracks drag distance, calculates visible percent and overlay opacity, manages open/close transitions, and wires all pointer/touch event listeners.
nodes.languageNotes: Handles both touch and mouse events, including scroll-lock detection for swiper slides, with configurable close threshold and pixel offset.

nodes.name: useBottomSheetDrag
nodes.summary: Main composable function returning drag state, computed styles, and event management functions for animating and controlling bottom sheet open/close via pointer gestures.

nodes.name: EditModal.vue
nodes.summary: Bottom sheet modal wrapper for category create/edit that initializes the form from the categories store and delegates to CategoriesForm.

nodes.name: Form.vue
nodes.summary: Full-featured category create/edit form handling name, icon, color, parent selection, and hierarchical child management with realtime sync safety, Zod validation, and Convex persistence.
nodes.languageNotes: Manages child category selection with a guarded sync watcher to avoid clobbering user in-progress state when realtime patches arrive from another device.

nodes.name: Header.vue
nodes.summary: Category detail page header showing breadcrumb navigation with a clickable parent category link and the current category name.

nodes.name: Item.vue
nodes.summary: Category list item component that optionally wraps CategoriesItemBody in a context menu and forwards all interaction events.

nodes.name: ItemBody.vue
nodes.summary: Detailed body for a single category row, rendering icon, name, optional parent breadcrumb, expand chevron, and a context menu or dropdown menu button depending on display mode.

nodes.name: List.vue
nodes.summary: Reusable categories list component that renders a tree of root and child category items with support for expand/collapse, grid/list child views, context menus, and click handling.

nodes.name: Name.vue
nodes.summary: Presentational component that renders a category name with optional children count badge and parent category label.

nodes.name: Edit.vue
nodes.summary: Category edit page that loads the existing category by route param, renders the CategoriesForm, and navigates back after save.

nodes.name: Index.vue
nodes.summary: Category detail page showing full statistics for the category with child category list, stat tabs, date range config, delete confirmation flow for both the parent and individual children, and context menu actions.

nodes.name: List.vue
nodes.summary: Categories listing page with expand/collapse all, list/grid view toggle, context menus per category, and delete confirmation modal with transaction count warning.

nodes.name: New.vue
nodes.summary: New category creation page that renders CategoriesForm and supports both onboarding and standard navigation flows after save.

nodes.name: RoundLink.vue
nodes.summary: Wrapper that renders a CategoriesRoundPill inside a NuxtLink with optional context menu, used for grid-style child category navigation.

nodes.name: RoundPill.vue
nodes.summary: Pill-style category badge showing icon, name, and optional parent label with color-tinted background and active state styling.

nodes.name: SelectorGrid.vue
nodes.summary: Grid-layout category selector that renders CategoriesItem tiles and an optional "New" button, emitting a selected event on leaf category click.

nodes.name: SelectorModal.vue
nodes.summary: Full-featured category selector panel with search, list/grid toggle, expand/collapse, favorites/recent filter, context menus, delete confirmation, and inline or routed editing - used as the primary category picker in the transaction form.

nodes.name: SelectorTree.vue
nodes.summary: Tree-style category selector with search, expand/collapse, checkbox multi-selection per root and child, and list/grid child view - used for stat filter category selection.

nodes.name: StatPage.vue
nodes.summary: Reusable category statistics page that wires up stat filter, date range, and config contexts for a given category route param and renders StatHeader and StatWrap.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: TypeScript types and Zod validation schema for category data - defines CategoryItem, CategoryForm, Categories map type, and the Zod parse schema with defaults.

nodes.name: useCategoriesExpanded.ts
nodes.summary: Composable that manages persistent expand/collapse state for a set of category IDs using localStorage, returning toggle, toggleAll, isExpanded, and a dynamic folderIcon computed.

nodes.name: useCategoriesExpanded
nodes.summary: Composable that persists per-category expand state to localStorage and exposes toggle/toggleAll/isExpanded helpers and a reactive folderIcon.

nodes.name: useCategoriesStore.ts
nodes.summary: Pinia store for categories providing full CRUD with optimistic updates, offline queue, Convex sync, realtime patch application, and derived computeds for transactible, favorite, and recent category IDs.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Pinia defineStore with the composable setup pattern. Implements a two-phase save (optimistic local update + Convex mutation) with reparent rollback for parent-child reorganization.

nodes.name: useCategoryMenuItems.ts
nodes.summary: Composable and helper that builds context menu item arrays for categories - providing open, edit, delete, and quick-toggle (favorite/recent) actions.

nodes.name: useCategoryMenuItems
nodes.summary: Returns an object of factory functions (delete, edit, open, quickToggles) that produce context menu item descriptors for a given category ID.

nodes.name: isMenuableCategory
nodes.summary: Guard that returns false for the built-in 'transfer' and 'adjustment' categories which must not show user-facing context menus.

nodes.name: utils.ts
nodes.summary: Pure utility functions for category data - including transactible ID resolution, parent lookup, name/parent-based comparators, and children diff computation.

nodes.name: getTransactibleCategoriesIds
nodes.summary: Resolves which category IDs are transactible: for root categories with children it returns the children, otherwise returns the category itself; skips duplicates via a seen set.

nodes.name: computeChildrenDiff
nodes.summary: Computes the symmetric difference between two arrays of child IDs, returning added and removed arrays.

nodes.name: colors.ts
nodes.summary: Static color palette data exporting a flat array of all Tailwind-like color hex values and a grouped array without shades - used as the default color picker dataset.
nodes.languageNotes: Entirely static data; disables perfectionist sort rule to preserve Tailwind color ordering.

nodes.name: Palette.vue
nodes.summary: Color palette picker component that renders grouped color swatches with optional category icon and wallet usage indicators, plus a custom hex color input.

nodes.name: currencies.ts

nodes.name: List.vue

nodes.name: List.vue

nodes.name: useCurrenciesStore.ts
nodes.summary: Pinia store that manages currency exchange rates, fetching the latest rates from Convex and persisting them to localforage for offline access.

nodes.name: useCurrencyName.ts

nodes.name: useCurrencyName
nodes.summary: Returns a getCurrencyName helper that resolves a currency code to its locale-aware display name.

nodes.name: LinkItem.vue

nodes.name: LinkItemNoBg.vue

nodes.name: Nav.vue

nodes.name: Ranges.vue

nodes.name: statDateParams.ts
nodes.summary: Provides default stat date parameters, a Zod-validated URL query param parser, and a date range computation function that resolves custom, max-range, or calculated intervals.

nodes.name: parseStatDateQueryParams
nodes.summary: Parses URL query string parameters via a Zod schema and merges valid fields into the current StatDateParams object.

nodes.name: computeDateRange
nodes.summary: Computes the active date range from StatDateParams with priority: custom date > max range > calculated interval.

nodes.name: Trns.vue
nodes.summary: Vue component that renders a date label linking to a filtered transactions list for a specific date period.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Core type definitions for the date/statistics subsystem, including Period, Range, StatDateParams, and the StatDateProvider return type alias.

nodes.name: useDateFormats.ts
nodes.summary: Composable that provides locale-aware date formatting helpers for the current stat date range, producing human-readable labels for different period granularities.

nodes.name: useDateFormats
nodes.summary: Returns formatDate and getDates helpers that format stat date ranges according to the active locale and period granularity.

nodes.name: useStatDate.ts
nodes.summary: Central composable managing stat date state - persists params to localStorage, computes intervals in range, and exposes actions for modifying the active date selection.

nodes.name: useStatDate
nodes.summary: Manages the full lifecycle of stat date parameters - initializes from storage and URL params, computes ranges and intervals, and provides mutation methods for UI controls.

nodes.name: utils.ts
nodes.summary: Low-level date utility functions for interval computation, period boundary calculation, and calendar date parsing, wrapping date-fns and the @internationalized/date library.

nodes.name: calculateIntervalInRange
nodes.summary: Calculates the start and end timestamps for a single interval within a date range based on duration and period type.

nodes.name: getIntervalsInRange
nodes.summary: Generates a list of all interval objects covering a given date range, adjusting boundary intervals to match the range exactly.

nodes.name: getStartOf

nodes.name: getEndOf
nodes.summary: Returns the end-of-period Date for a given date and period type (year, month, week, or day).

nodes.name: calculateBestIntervalsBy
nodes.summary: Determines the most appropriate interval granularity (day/week/month/year) for a given date range based on its total duration in days.

nodes.summary: Static lookup map of 169 ISO 4217 currency codes to their exchange rate values, used to seed the demo mode with realistic multi-currency data.

nodes.name: data.ts
nodes.summary: Static fixture data for demo mode, defining sample wallets, categories, and rule sets (expense/income/transfer) with weighted probabilities used by the demo data generator.

nodes.name: useDemo.ts

nodes.name: useDemo

nodes.name: SidebarMenu.vue
nodes.summary: Renders the list of navigation menu items for the sidebar, supporting both icon-only and icon-with-text display modes.

nodes.name: SidebarMenuItem.vue

nodes.name: useMenuData.ts

nodes.name: useMenuData
nodes.summary: Builds the full app navigation menu data including localized labels, active state per route, and grouped item sets for the sidebar and bottom bar.

nodes.name: Switcher.vue
nodes.summary: UI component for switching the application locale, presenting available locales as selectable options with flag icons.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Exports the Zod schema `localeSchema` used to validate locale values across the locale feature.

nodes.name: Tabs.ts
nodes.summary: Exports the `tabsNavUi` configuration object providing shared UI styling tokens for tab-based navigation components.

nodes.name: helpers.ts
nodes.summary: Core offline queue management module providing serialized read/write operations to localforage for queued mutations, supporting push, remove, update, and clear operations with a write-chain concurrency guard.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a write-chain promise pattern to serialize all queue mutations and prevent race conditions in concurrent offline operations.

nodes.name: pushOfflineOp
nodes.summary: Enqueues a new offline operation by chaining onto the write promise to serialize queue access.

nodes.name: pushOfflineOpImpl
nodes.summary: Implements the actual push logic: reads the queue, detects create/update/delete coalescing opportunities to compact redundant operations, then writes back.

nodes.name: removeOfflineOp
nodes.summary: Removes a single offline operation by entity type and ID, serialized via the write chain.

nodes.name: updateOfflineOpData
nodes.summary: Updates the data payload of an existing offline operation by entity and ID, serialized via the write chain.

nodes.name: clearOfflineQueue
nodes.summary: Clears all items from the offline queue in localforage, serialized via the write chain.

nodes.name: replay.ts
nodes.summary: Orchestrates replay of the offline operation queue after reconnection, iterating stored ops and dispatching them to wallets, categories, transactions, and settings stores in dependency order.

nodes.name: replayOfflineQueue
nodes.summary: Main replay orchestrator: validates user identity, groups queued ops by entity type, and executes each group through its corresponding replay helper in the correct order.

nodes.name: replayHelpers.ts
nodes.summary: Provides entity-specific replay functions (wallets, categories, transactions, settings) plus shared helpers for validation, ID remapping, orphan detection, and operation grouping used during offline queue replay.

nodes.name: replayWalletOps
nodes.summary: Replays queued wallet create/update/delete operations, collecting server ID remappings for later use by dependent entities.

nodes.name: replayCategoryOps
nodes.summary: Replays queued category operations in two passes (parent categories first, then children) to respect parent-child dependencies, remapping IDs as needed.

nodes.name: replayTrnOps
nodes.summary: Replays queued transaction operations, remapping wallet and category IDs and skipping orphaned transactions whose referenced entities were deleted.

nodes.name: groupOpsByEntity
nodes.summary: Partitions a flat list of offline operations into separate arrays by entity type (wallet, category, transaction, settings, unknown).

nodes.name: storageKeys.ts
nodes.summary: Exports the `STORAGE_KEYS` constant object defining all localforage key names used by the offline queue system.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the `OfflineEntityType` union type and related TypeScript interfaces for the offline operation queue system.

nodes.name: SearchCommandPalette.vue
nodes.summary: Command palette component providing grouped search results across navigation items, categories, wallets, and transactions with keyboard navigation support.

nodes.name: SearchModal.vue
nodes.summary: Modal wrapper that presents the SearchCommandPalette in an overlay with search input binding and open/close state management.

nodes.name: useSearch.ts
nodes.summary: Composable that aggregates searchable items from navigation, categories, wallets, and transactions into grouped, filterable lists with recent-items tracking per entity type.

nodes.name: useSearch
nodes.summary: Builds computed search item groups for navigation, categories, wallets, and transactions, and exposes recent-items lists with configurable limits.

nodes.name: Average.vue
nodes.summary: Statistics component that displays average income, expense, and balance figures computed over the current filter period.

nodes.name: barUtils.ts
nodes.summary: Utility functions for category bar charts: computing dynamic bar width/height CSS styles relative to the max value, and formatting amounts into compact K/M notation.

nodes.name: computeBarStyle
nodes.summary: Returns inline CSS style object setting bar color and width/height percentage relative to the maximum category value.

nodes.name: formatCompactAmount
nodes.summary: Formats a numeric amount into a compact string with M (millions) or K (thousands) suffix for display in bar chart labels.

nodes.name: collectAndGroup.ts
nodes.summary: Pure functions for aggregating transaction IDs by category and grouping them into parent/child category hierarchies with computed monetary values.

nodes.name: collectCategoriesByTrns
nodes.summary: Iterates filtered transactions and accumulates transaction IDs per category, building a map of category ID to transaction ID list.

nodes.name: groupCategoriesWithValues
nodes.summary: Groups flat category entries into a parent-child hierarchy, aggregating values up to parent level and sorting each group by amount.

nodes.name: DetailedSection.vue
nodes.summary: Large statistics section component showing a detailed breakdown of spending/income by category with expandable parent-child groups and transaction drill-down.

nodes.name: GroupingToggle.vue
nodes.summary: Small toggle button component that switches the category statistics view between grouped (parent/child) and flat display modes.

nodes.name: Line.vue
nodes.summary: Category statistics row component rendering a horizontal bar chart line with category name, color, and relative amount for a single category entry.

nodes.name: Round.vue
nodes.summary: Category statistics component rendering a single category as a colored circular chip with its name and amount for use in round-style summaries.

nodes.name: RoundRecent.vue
nodes.summary: Displays a horizontally scrollable row of recently used category chips for quick access in the statistics view.

nodes.name: RoundSection.vue
nodes.summary: Section wrapper that renders a grid of Round category chips grouped by type (income/expense) for the round-style statistics view.

nodes.name: useCategoryLongPress.ts
nodes.summary: Composable that attaches a long-press handler to a category element: long-press opens the transaction creation form pre-filled with that category, while a short press invokes the provided callback.

nodes.name: useCategoryLongPress
nodes.summary: Binds a VueUse onLongPress handler to a category element ref, opening the transaction form with the category pre-selected on long press.

nodes.name: useStatCategories.ts

nodes.name: useStatCategories
nodes.summary: Composes collectCategoriesByTrns, groupCategoriesWithValues, and flattenCategoriesWithValues with store data to produce ready-to-render category stats.

nodes.name: Vertical.vue
nodes.summary: Category statistics component rendering category entries as vertical bar charts suitable for portrait-oriented layouts.

nodes.name: config.ts
nodes.summary: Exports static ECharts configuration objects for the statistics chart, including per-series color/type options, base grid/tooltip/axis config, and default series styling used by the chart renderer.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ECharts ComposeOption and CSS custom properties for theming, with formatter callbacks referencing sibling utils.

nodes.name: Intervals.vue
nodes.summary: Dropdown selector that computes and renders the available time-interval options (day/week/month/year) for the chart based on the current date range size, emitting a changePeriod event when the user switches.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the ChartType union type ('bar' | 'line') shared across chart components and composables.

nodes.name: useCategorySeriesBuilder.ts
nodes.summary: Provides the buildCategoriesSeries pure function that aggregates transaction IDs per category per interval, resolves parent grouping, and returns sorted ChartSeries data for ECharts.

nodes.name: buildCategoriesSeries
nodes.summary: Builds a sorted array of ChartSeries from transaction data, bucketing trns by category and interval, computing totals, and ordering by descending total.

nodes.name: useStatChart.ts
nodes.summary: Composable that provides chart-type option labels, a createSeriesItem helper for building expense/income series with optional average mark-line, and a withMarkArea helper for marking a selected date on the chart.

nodes.name: useStatChart
nodes.summary: Vue composable returning chartTypeOptions (computed), createSeriesItem (builds a single typed series with mark-line), and withMarkArea (injects a mark-area band into series for a highlighted date).

nodes.name: utils.ts
nodes.summary: Pure utility functions for ECharts chart formatting: createMarkAreaData builds a highlight band definition, formatCompactChartAmount formats numbers with compact notation, and formatChartAmount uses a locale-aware Intl.NumberFormat with memoization.

nodes.name: formatChartAmount
nodes.summary: Formats a numeric amount as a locale-aware integer string, caching Intl.NumberFormat instances per locale in a module-level Map.

nodes.name: View.vue
nodes.summary: The core ECharts chart rendering component that initializes bar/line/mark-area/mark-line renderers, computes the full chart option from config and series props, and emits a click event with the interval index for date selection.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses vue-echarts VChart with SVGRenderer and defu deep-merge to combine static config with runtime series data.

nodes.name: Wrap.vue
nodes.summary: Wrapper component that conditionally renders the chart area when the user has chart display enabled, composing Intervals and lazy-loaded View with statConfig/statDate injections and forwarding click events to update the trnForm date.

nodes.name: ConfigModal.vue
nodes.summary: Modal/dropdown container for the statistics configuration panel, providing the BottomSheetOrDropdown trigger (settings icon or label button) and injecting statConfigPanelKey so nested panels share active-panel state.

nodes.name: ConfigPanelHeader.vue
nodes.summary: Sticky back-navigation header used at the top of every configuration sub-panel, showing the panel title and emitting a back event on click or keyboard activation.

nodes.name: CatsList.vue
nodes.summary: Config sub-panel for the categories list view, exposing toggle switches for isLines and isRoundIcon options via StatConfigSwitch.

nodes.name: CatsRound.vue
nodes.summary: Config sub-panel for the round categories view, providing toggles for parent-group aggregation, favorites display, recent display, and icon background rounding.

nodes.name: Chart.vue
nodes.summary: Config sub-panel for chart settings, offering switches for quick-date display, average line, category mode, parent grouping, chart view layout (half/full), and chart type (bar/line).

nodes.name: Grouping.vue
nodes.summary: Config sub-panel that lets the user select the global category grouping mode (auto/flat/grouped) using a pill tab bar.

nodes.name: StatAverage.vue
nodes.summary: Config sub-panel for the statistics average period, showing a number stepper to set the average rolling window count.

nodes.name: Vertical.vue
nodes.summary: Config sub-panel for the vertical categories list, providing a single toggle for parent-group aggregation.

nodes.name: Wallets.vue
nodes.summary: Config sub-panel for the wallets view, providing a number stepper bound to the wallets.count config with a max derived from the sorted wallets list.

nodes.name: ConfigPanelToggle.vue
nodes.summary: Reusable toggle row for config sub-panel headers, rendering an On/Off switch with an optional description line; conditionally hidden when value is undefined.

nodes.name: ConfigRow.vue
nodes.summary: Accessible list-row component for the root config panel, combining a navigable label area (with optional chevron for sub-panels) and a separated toggle switch, emitting activate and toggle events independently.

nodes.name: ConfigSwitch.vue
nodes.summary: Generic config switch component that reads and toggles a nested boolean field on a statConfig key, delegating rendering to UiSwitchItem.

nodes.name: ConfigView.vue
nodes.summary: Top-level statistics configuration view that orchestrates the root panel list and all sub-panels with animated slide transitions (motion-v), using inject/provide for shared panel state and computing available panels from props.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses motion-v AnimatePresence with custom direction variants for slide-in/slide-out transitions and VueUse useElementSize to maintain stable panel height during animation.

nodes.name: Navigation.vue
nodes.summary: Date navigation bar for the statistics view, rendering prev/next/today navigation arrows and a BottomSheetOrDropdown date-selector trigger, with computed flags for boundary and home-button visibility.

nodes.name: Quick.vue
nodes.summary: Compact horizontal scrollable quick-select bar showing period and preset date range buttons, with an expand toggle to reveal additional preset groups.

nodes.name: Range.vue

nodes.name: Selector.vue
nodes.summary: Full date selector panel with a presets tab (period ranges, presets, maximum range, and interval grouping controls) and a calendar tab (UCalendar range picker), covering all date selection modes for the statistics view.

nodes.name: useGetDateRange.ts

nodes.languageNotes: Nested inner functions (formatYearRange, formatMonthRange, formatWeekRange, formatDayRange) are kept private inside the exported composable, following a closure-based encapsulation pattern common in Vue composables.

nodes.name: useGetDateRange
nodes.summary: Factory composable that closes over a translation function and locale to produce three formatting utilities: formatDateToString, formatDateToStringWithLast, and getStringDateRange.

nodes.name: Selected.vue

nodes.name: Selector.vue

nodes.name: SelectorItem.vue
nodes.summary: Reusable trigger-and-panel component for a single filter dimension (wallets or categories), renders an icon button with an active chip indicator and a bottom-sheet/dropdown containing the selector content.

nodes.name: types.ts

nodes.name: useFilter.ts

nodes.name: useFilter
nodes.summary: Exported composable that wires up wallet and category query filters backed by URL query parameters and exposes a unified filter API with clear, toggle, set, and remove operations.

nodes.name: Header.vue
nodes.summary: Stat page header component showing the page title, navigation tabs, filter selectors, config modal trigger, and an optional wallet strip, all wired to injected filter/config/date providers.

nodes.name: injectionKeys.ts

nodes.languageNotes: bucketTrnsByIntervals uses binary search to assign each transaction to its interval in O(N log M) time, avoiding the naive O(N*M) nested-loop approach.

nodes.name: bucketTrnsByIntervals

nodes.name: computeAverageTotal
nodes.summary: Computes per-day, per-week, and per-month averages for a given sum over a date range, returning only the granularities that span more than one unit.

nodes.name: isPeriodOneDay

nodes.name: Menu.vue

nodes.name: Item.vue

nodes.name: Calculator.vue

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Exports the TrnFormUi type describing the form's UI visibility state.

nodes.summary: Safe recursive-descent arithmetic evaluator that parses and computes +/-/* // expressions with correct operator precedence, returning 0 on invalid input or overflow.

nodes.name: createExpressionString

nodes.name: formatInput

nodes.name: formatTransfer

nodes.name: validate.ts
nodes.summary: Validates a Transaction or Transfer using the Zod trnItemSchema and returns a localized i18n error key string, or null when valid.

nodes.name: validate
nodes.summary: Runs safeParse against trnItemSchema and maps individual Zod issue paths to human-readable i18n error keys for the transaction form.

nodes.name: ActionButton.vue

nodes.name: ButtonAccent.vue

nodes.name: ContextMenuOptional.vue

nodes.name: Element.vue

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: README.md is the best entry point for understanding Finapp's purpose and scope. It explains the key features - offline-first PWA, real-time Convex sync, multi-currency support, and the full transaction model - and maps out the pnpm monorepo structure with its two workspace packages: app/ (Nuxt SPA + Convex backend) and docs/. Reading this first gives you the mental model that shapes every architectural decision you will encounter in the steps ahead.

tour.title: App Entry Point
tour.description: app.vue is the root Vue component that Nuxt mounts for every page. It configures global head metadata and SEO tags, injects CSS variables for theming (primary color, border radius), sets up the global toast configuration, and runs the auth guard that decides whether to redirect unauthenticated visitors to /login. Everything the user sees flows through this component first.
tour.languageLesson: In Nuxt 4, app.vue is the universal root - it wraps every layout and page via <NuxtLayout> and <NuxtPage>. Unlike layout files, app.vue runs for every route without exception, making it the right place for truly global concerns like auth guards, head metadata, and design-token CSS variables.

tour.title: Nuxt Configuration
tour.description: nuxt.config.ts is the project's build and runtime manifest. It registers all key modules - @nuxt/ui (Tailwind v4 components), @pinia/nuxt, @vite-pwa/nuxt, @nuxtjs/i18n, and @vueuse/nuxt - configures the Workbox service worker for offline caching, sets SPA mode (ssr: false), and exposes the Convex deployment URL as a public runtime config. The PWA manifest and reka-ui chunk-isolation hints defined here directly enable the offline-first and performance goals from the README.
tour.languageLesson: Nuxt's runtimeConfig separates build-time constants (baked into the bundle) from public runtime values (readable by client code via useRuntimeConfig()). CONVEX_URL lives here so the same build artifact can be pointed at different Convex deployments without rebuilding.

tour.title: Convex Schema & Domain Types
tour.description: schema.ts defines every database table Finapp uses - categories, wallets, trns, userSettings, rates, syncMeta, and tombstones - with their field validators and composite indexes. validators.ts complements it by exporting the WalletType union and the TrnType enum (Expense=0, Income=1, Transfer=2) that are imported by every backend mutation and most frontend stores. Together these two files form the canonical data contract between the Convex backend and the Nuxt frontend.
tour.languageLesson: Convex uses a TypeScript-native schema DSL where every table field is declared with a validator (v.string(), v.number(), v.union(...)) that is enforced at runtime on the Convex cloud. The schema generates TypeScript types used by all query/mutation wrappers, giving end-to-end type safety from database to UI without a code-generation step.

tour.title: Authentication Layer
tour.description: auth.config.ts registers Better Auth as Convex's auth provider, auth.ts configures the Better Auth instance with Google OAuth, encrypted tokens, and a 30-day session lifetime, and http.ts wires the Better Auth HTTP routes into the Convex HTTP router with CORS enabled. On the frontend, the convex.ts Nuxt plugin initializes the ConvexClient, fetches auth tokens from Better Auth, and handles online/offline transitions. This three-file chain is the bridge between the SPA and the serverless backend.
tour.languageLesson: Better Auth's Convex plugin stores session tokens inside the Convex database, so auth state is durable and queryable server-side. The cross-domain plugin adds the necessary cookie forwarding for SPA deployments where the frontend origin differs from the Convex deployment URL.

tour.title: Convex Client & Realtime Bridge
tour.description: client.ts in app/services/convex/ creates the singleton ConvexClient (a WebSocket connection to the Convex cloud). useConvex.ts wraps it in two composables: useConvexClient() for raw access and useConvexClientWithApi() which pairs the client with the typed api object so stores can call client.query(api.trns.list) with full TypeScript inference. useRealtimeSync.ts uses these composables to open per-entity delta subscriptions and forward incoming patches to the Pinia stores.
tour.languageLesson: Convex's client.onUpdate() callback fires every time the server pushes a reactive update. Finapp uses delta sync - the server sends only the changed IDs and a hash rather than the full dataset - so the WebSocket traffic stays small even with thousands of transactions.

tour.title: Core Backend Modules
tour.description: trns.ts, wallets.ts, and categories.ts are the three primary Convex backend modules. Each implements full CRUD, real-time delta sync, and hash-based integrity checks. shared.ts provides the ownership-checked entity loader and domain validation constants that all three modules depend on. trnsHash.ts maintains a rolling FNV-1a XOR hash of all transaction IDs per user so the client can detect divergence with a single number comparison rather than fetching the whole list.
tour.languageLesson: FNV-1a XOR hashing is an O(1) sync check: each client stores the same rolling hash and compares it to the server's value on reconnect. A mismatch triggers a full re-sync; a match means the local state is already current. This avoids expensive set-difference calculations across thousands of IDs.

tour.title: Pinia Stores - State Management
tour.description: Each major domain entity has its own Pinia store that mirrors the Convex backend. useTrnsStore.ts, useWalletsStore.ts, useCategoriesStore.ts, and useUserStore.ts each implement the same pattern: load from localStorage cache on startup for instant UI, perform a full sync from Convex on first connection, then apply realtime delta patches as they arrive. useStoreSync.ts is the shared synchronization helper they all call to manage the offline operation queue and resolve optimistic IDs once Convex confirms a create.
tour.languageLesson: The stores follow an optimistic-update pattern: a mutation is applied to local state immediately and enqueued to the offline queue, then sent to Convex. If Convex succeeds, the temporary client-generated ID is remapped to the server-assigned ID. If the app is offline, the operation stays in the queue and replays on reconnect.

tour.title: Transaction Domain Model & Form
tour.description: types.ts defines the core TrnType enum, Zod schemas for Transaction and Transfer objects, and the guard functions that distinguish them at runtime - making it the vocabulary file every form and stat component refers to. useTrnsFormStore.ts is the most widely imported store in the project (fan-in: 17) and manages the entire transaction creation/edit flow: the built-in calculator, wallet and category selection, transfer-mode logic, and optimistic Convex persistence on submit.
tour.languageLesson: Zod schemas serve a dual purpose here: they validate incoming data at runtime (catching bad Convex responses or offline-queue corruption) and they generate TypeScript types via z.infer<>, eliminating duplicate interface declarations. The safe recursive-descent evaluator in calculate.ts avoids eval() for security while still supporting +, -, *, / expressions.

tour.title: Main Layout & Default Page
tour.description: default.vue is the application shell that wraps every authenticated page. It bootstraps data loading from both localStorage cache and Convex, manages the onboarding wizard for new users, renders the persistent sidebar and bottom navigation menu, mounts the floating transaction form, and registers global keyboard shortcuts. dashboard.vue is the default destination after login and shows either the StatPage for users with data or the AppOnboarding wizard for new accounts.

tour.title: Analytics & Statistics Engine
tour.description: The stat subsystem is the most architecturally complex area of the frontend. useStatPage.ts bootstraps the three stat providers (filter, config, date) via Vue's provide/inject pattern, and useStatItem.ts is the core analytics composable that computes chart series, interval buckets, rolling averages, and category breakdowns for a single stat view. injectionKeys.ts defines typed injection keys so all stat child components share the same reactive context without prop drilling.
tour.languageLesson: Vue's provide/inject is used here as a scoped dependency injection system: useStatPage.ts calls provide(filterKey, useFilter()) so all deeply nested chart and category components can inject the same reactive filter object without threading it through multiple layers of props. The typed injection keys in injectionKeys.ts prevent type mismatches at inject() call sites.

tour.title: Offline-First Sync Architecture
tour.description: The offline subsystem is what makes Finapp work without internet. helpers.ts manages serialized read/write access to the localforage offline queue. When the app reconnects, replay.ts iterates the queued operations in dependency order (wallets before categories before transactions) and dispatches them to the appropriate store. replayHelpers.ts handles the hard cases - ID remapping, orphan detection when a referenced wallet was deleted while offline, and operation grouping to minimize API calls. tombstones.ts on the backend tracks soft-deleted IDs so clients that reconnect after being offline for days can reconcile without gaps.
tour.languageLesson: Tombstones solve the offline delete problem: if client A deletes a wallet while offline and client B adds a transaction to it in the meantime, the tombstone record lets the replay engine detect and resolve the conflict rather than silently creating an orphaned transaction. The daily cron purges tombstones older than 30 days, which is the maximum offline window the app guarantees to support.
